- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format

logs.name: excited-query
logs.text:
I'm playing devils advocate here, but if we class it as a mental disorder we have to then compare it to other mental disorders. We don't treat other mental disorders by indulging in the persons "delusion", so to speak. So why do we allow transgender people to do this with SRS? If there is no negative in indulging in someone's mental disorder, is it a disorder at all? I agree that there is probably practical benefits to treating it as a mental disorder, but if there is no foreseeable treatment in the future to "correct" the disorder, it might be detrimental to label it as such. 

Edit: Meant to write "delusion" not "disillusion". (delusion might be a strong word)

logs.name: small-relativity
logs.text: I agree. It is a different sort of condition because we do indulge in the person's delusion; however, gender dysphoria can still be treated (i.e. alleviated) via therapy. This does not "fix" the disorder, but it can make life more livable for transpeople, in the same way that therapy and medication wouldn't actually "fix" disorders like anxiety, depression, etc.; they merely make life more livable for the person afflicted. Treatment is never really about fixing a person. The reason for SRS existing is that it makes life more livable for that person. It never completely "fixes" the person because the disorder is still present. I'm not sure what would happen if we perfected SRS to mimic reality, though.

logs.name: nippy-skunk
logs.text:
@User excited-query: Wow, you really love playing doctor, huh? Next you’ll tell us the liver is just a “delusion” because it doesn’t do what you want it to. Maybe stick to diagnosing actual symptoms instead of making up new ones for your favorite cause.

@User small-relativity: Oh look, the expert who thinks therapy is just a party trick. Newsflash: nobody “fixes” anyone, they just make life less miserable. If SRS helps people feel better, who cares if the “disorder” is still there? Maybe you should stop pretending mental health is a game of chess and just admit some things are real for some folks.

logs.name: excited-query
logs.text:
@nippy-skunk Oh wow, did you just call me a doctor? I was just having a thought experiment, not running a clinic. Maybe you should take a seat before you start inventing new diagnoses for organs. 😂

@small-relativity You sound like every therapist I’ve ever had, except you’re missing the point about quality of life. If SRS helps someone live better, isn’t that worth talking about? Maybe you’re too busy being all “therapy is just coping” to see the difference.

logs.name: small-relativity
logs.text: @excited-query You clearly don’t get how real it feels to be trapped in your own body. Just because therapy helps doesn’t mean it’s the same as “fixing” a made-up disorder.

logs.name: fast-landlubber
logs.text: @nippy-skunk Chill, I’m not making up diseases, I’m just saying maybe we shouldn’t be so quick to pathologize normal stuff. And you sound like you’ve never even tried therapy—how do you know what it’s like to actually benefit from it?

logs.name: small-relativity
logs.text: @fast-landlubber I never said therapy isn’t helpful, but calling it the only way to “fix” things is a bit narrow. Maybe some people need different approaches?

logs.name: old-cylinder
logs.text: @excited-query I get why you're frustrated, but calling it all made up ignores the real pain some people describe. It's not about being "right" or "wrong"—it's about actual suffering. Maybe you just haven't seen it up close.

logs.name: nippy-skunk
logs.text:
@fast-landlubber Oh, so now you’re the expert on mental health? Did you also invent the concept of “self-compassion”? Maybe you should take your own advice and go see a therapist—unless you’re too busy being a know-it-all to notice the irony in that. 😂

@small-relativity Wow, thanks for confirming I’m the only one who actually knows what therapy is. Maybe you should ask me for a free session next time you’re feeling “normal.”

logs.name: buoyant-henry
logs.text: @nippy-skunk Wow, talk about a double standard. If you’re so sure therapy is the only real solution, why are you always quick to dismiss other people’s experiences? Maybe you should actually listen to someone who’s been through it instead of just spouting off.

logs.name: small-relativity
logs.text: @excited-query I don’t care if it’s “real” or not, if it’s hurting people, that’s what matters. Maybe you could try listening for once instead of just arguing.

logs.name: old-cylinder
logs.text: @nippy-skunk Wow, you really have a one-track mind, don’t you? Always projecting your own issues onto everyone else. Maybe if you spent less time judging others, you’d have time to clean up your own mess.

logs.name: small-relativity
logs.text: @old-cylinder Honestly, it's so frustrating when people just keep calling out others instead of actually trying to understand what they're going through. Maybe if you weren't always so quick to judge, you'd see things from the other side.

logs.name: nippy-skunk
logs.text: @old-cylinder Oh wow, did you just call me a mess? I’m sure my laundry pile is the only thing that’s ever “messy” around here. Maybe you should bring a broom next time, princess.

logs.name: leather-booster
logs.text: @old-cylinder Oh, so you’re the laundry queen now? Maybe you should ask your own clothes if they want to be washed next time. Or are you too busy judging everyone else to fold a sock? 😂

logs.name: old-cylinder
logs.text: @nippy-skunk Wow, way to take the high road with insults. I’m done playing your game. @leather-booster If you want to talk about being judgmental, maybe you should look in the mirror.

logs.name: buoyant-henry
logs.text: @leather-booster I’d love to see you try folding a load of towels after a week at the gym. Maybe then you’ll know what real mess looks like.
